info.name: 2L1nk HTTP API
info.description:
REST API for gate access, user management, and chat lifecycle.
All endpoints are under /api.

Authentication and authenticity model:
- The gate key is used only for the initial gate authorization step.
- All requests are signed by the client using the same canonical signing method.
- After gate authorization, authenticated endpoints also require a session header.

Canonical signature input (same for all endpoints):
  METHOD + "\n" +
  PATH + "\n" +
  TIMESTAMP + "\n" +
  BODY_HASH

Where:
- METHOD is the uppercase HTTP method, for example POST
- PATH is the exact request path, for example /api/auth/gate
- TIMESTAMP is the value of the request header Chat-Timestamp
- BODY_HASH is the lowercase hex SHA-256 hash of the exact raw request body bytes

Example canonical string:
  POST
  /api/rooms
  1741262400
  8f434346648f6b96df89dda901c5176b10a6d83961fca65a509cf3307f10db86

Signature transport:
- Chat-Timestamp header carries the Unix timestamp
- Chat-Signature header carries the base64-encoded signature of the canonical string

Verification rules:
- Server rejects stale timestamps outside the allowed clock skew window
- Server reconstructs the canonical string from the incoming request
- Server verifies the signature using:
  - the public key in the auth request for /api/auth/gate
  - the public key already bound to the session for authenticated endpoints

item.name: Authorize with gate key, prove key ownership, receive session ID

header.description: Unix timestamp used as part of the canonical signing string.

Base64-encoded signature of the canonical request string:
METHOD + "
" + PATH + "
" + TIMESTAMP + "
" + BODY_HASH
